rationale.R1.1: Open-ended 'What do you think?' applied to a research excerpt plausibly asks whether the contribution claim (chemistry-agnostic, model-free SoH estimation) is coherent and well-scoped — making conceptual reconstruction central but not clearly load-bearing over other dims.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact mixes mechanistic claims (IC peak physics, graphite staging), architectural choices (TCN + attention), and generalization claims (cross-chemistry transfer) whose internal consistency is a natural target of 'What do you think?' scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: Performance numbers (1.8% vs 3.4/2.9/2.5% RMSE) and the peak-correlation claim are specific quantitative assertions; assessing whether the experimental setup actually supports them is a plausible load-bearing reading of the delegation.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty vs. prior BMS-SoH literature (Plett 2004, Richardson 2017, bi-LSTM) is directly at stake in any 'what do you think about this paper' framing, placing delta assessment as potentially load-bearing alongside R1.2/R1.3.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for an assessment of the scientific and technical merit of the claims (validity of results, novelty vs. prior work, evidence quality), or a critique of how the work is communicated and argued (logical structure, presentation clarity, writing)?
